reason: Both answers use nearly identical five-point frameworks (data feedback loops, workflow depth, distribution/trust, domain-specific evaluation, cost/margin engineering), both correctly reject model quality as ever having been a durable moat, and both close with a clear, actionable directive. A more explicitly ties "speed of shipping on commoditized models" to the iteration-speed advantage. B more forcefully states the model lead is "worth ~zero" and reframes the feature as table stakes. Neither has factual errors or padding, and overall quality/actionability is comparable.
